issues.title: Mixed-Use Development at 108 N Leon Street (124 units)
issues.summary: Consideration of a four-story mixed-use building with 124 dwelling units and 4,500 sq ft of commercial space in the CC-T District. Requires conditional use approval for reduced non-residential ground-floor frontage and unit count exceeding 60.

public_comment.instructions: Public hearing pending before Plan Commission; written comments to tparks@cityofmadison.com

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: This housing project directly supports Madison's Housing Forward plan (15,000 units by 2030) and addresses your concern about housing costs. As a low-engagement voter, you can observe how the city is addressing affordable housing through new development.

issues.title: Cherokee Heights Middle School Replacement Building (District 10)
issues.summary: Conditional use approval for construction of a new public school building to replace Cherokee Heights Middle School. Requires approval for school use and building size exceeding 10,000 sq ft in a residential district.

public_comment.instructions: Public hearing scheduled; written comments to tparks@cityofmadison.com

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Public Education is your top priority (85/100 salience). This project invests in school infrastructure, directly supporting your value of education. Most voters support school modernization, making this a low-conflict item worth monitoring.

issues.title: Sennett Middle School Replacement Building (District 15)
issues.summary: Conditional use approval for construction of a new Sennett Middle School building in a Campus-Institutional District. Requires approval for building construction without a full campus master plan.

public_comment.instructions: Public hearing scheduled; written comments to tparks@cityofmadison.com

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Another school infrastructure investment aligned with your education focus. Modern school facilities support student outcomes, which matters significantly to your political values.

issues.title: 2027 Public Health Madison & Dane County Budget Proposal
issues.governing_body: Board of Health for Madison and Dane County
issues.summary: Proposed budget for Public Health operations in 2027. Currently in referral stage for committee review and council deliberation.

public_comment.instructions: Budget hearings typically held during council review; contact Board of Health or your alder for details

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Healthcare access is your second-highest priority (80/100). Budget decisions determine public health funding and service levels. However, with low engagement appetite, you may want to monitor final outcomes rather than engage directly.

issues.title: Alliant Energy Center Exhibition Hall Additions (District 14)
issues.summary: Conditional use approval for auditorium additions to the Exhibition Hall at Alliant Energy Center. Requires approval for public facility expansion in Parks and Recreation District.

public_comment.instructions: Public hearing scheduled; written comments to tparks@cityofmadison.com

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Lower salience for you (Parks: 55/100, Economic Development: 40/100). This is a quality-of-life amenity expansion but not central to your core priorities.

issues.title: Residential Parking Permit Amendment (101-109 N Franklin, 510 E Wilson)
issues.summary: Planned Development District amendment to remove restrictions on residential parking permits for downtown mixed-use buildings. Addresses access and livability for residents in PD developments.

public_comment.instructions: Public hearing scheduled; written comments to tparks@cityofmadison.com

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Touches on housing policy and resident access. Your profile is persuadable on housing if framed around livability and equity, but this is a narrow technical issue unlikely to activate strong interest.

issues.title: Oscar Avenue Setback Ordinance Amendment (District 12)
issues.summary: Proposed amendment to remove part of a setback line on Oscar Avenue. Technical land-use adjustment to zoning code affecting commercial development constraints.

public_comment.instructions: Public hearing occurred on 2026-07-07; written comments to mglaeser@cityofmadison.com

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Technical zoning matter with limited direct impact on housing affordability or education. Low relevance to your stated priorities.

issues.title: Cherokee Heights Middle School Demolition Permit (District 10)
issues.summary: Demolition permit for the existing Cherokee Heights Middle School building. Already approved by Landmarks Commission as of 2026-07-17.
issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Part of school modernization (supporting your education priority), but the decision is already made. Only relevant if you want historical context on the school replacement project.

issues.title: Discussion of Alder and BCC Goals (Disability Rights Commission)
issues.summary: Commission discussion of goals and priorities for city alder and Board of Community Connections coordination. In-committee discussion item.

public_comment.instructions: In-committee discussion; no public comment period typically available

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Relevant to civil rights and equity (65/100 salience), but this is an internal commission discussion with limited direct impact. Worth monitoring if specific policies emerge.

issues.title: Accessible and Inclusive Emergency Planning (Disability Rights Commission)
issues.summary: Commission discussion on making emergency planning more accessible and inclusive for people with disabilities. In-committee item for ongoing deliberation.

public_comment.instructions: In-committee discussion; direct engagement unlikely unless item moves to public hearing

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Aligns with your collective/equity values and civil rights salience (65/100), but this is foundational policy work unlikely to require public input soon.

issues.title: Other Motorized Vehicles in Parks and Pedestrian Paths (Disability Rights Commission)
issues.summary: Commission discussion on policies governing motorized vehicles (mobility aids, e-scooters, etc.) in public parks and pedestrian paths. In-committee item.

public_comment.instructions: In-committee discussion; no immediate public engagement opportunity

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Touches equity and parks (both moderate salience for you), but framed as a disability access issue. Worth tracking if it produces concrete policy changes.

issues.title: Plan Commission Public Comment Period (Standing Procedure)
issues.summary: Standing agenda item for general public comment at Plan Commission meetings. Allows community members to address matters not otherwise listed on agenda.

public_comment.instructions: Public comment available at all Plan Commission meetings; contact tparks@cityofmadison.com for scheduling

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: This is a mechanism for your voice if you choose to engage on specific Plan Commission items. Low importance score reflects your low engagement appetite, but high impact if you decide to comment on housing or school projects.
